data_basis: PRODUCTION — market-analyst (DA energy prices, P(DA>RT), AS spike prices); MISSING — as-playbook

data_sources.energy_prices: market-analyst 2026-05-26.md (Smartbidder DA forecast via Yes Energy + Enverus + AG2, 24h values)
data_sources.p_da_gt_rt: market-analyst 2026-05-26.md: HE20-22 P(DA>RT) = 66-67% (Smartbidder composite); HE09-11 P(DA<RT) = 65-66%
data_sources.congestion: congestion-analyst 2026-05-26.md (Stage 0 heuristic): WEST_TO_NORTH LOW-MEDIUM 12-18% HE09-14; HOUSTON_IMPORT MEDIUM 20-28% HE18-21; No GKS LMP adjustment
data_sources.prior_actuals: pnl-manager 2026-05-24.md (Tenaska): NonSpin dominant (980 MW-hr), RRS HE19-24 pattern

assumptions.starting_soc_basis: Task-specified 50% DoD = 100 MWh. Operations must confirm via Tenaska PTP HSL/PMin before 10:00 CT.
assumptions.hsl_source: ASSUMED — operations to confirm from Tenaska PTP dataPoints=[HSL]
assumptions.as_prices_basis_offpeak: ESTIMATED from market-analyst seasonal pattern (off-peak near-zero $1-2/MWh per market-analyst 2026-05-26.md). Low confidence HE01-18.
assumptions.as_prices_basis_peak: PRODUCTION — Smartbidder via market-analyst: RRS HE19=$11.28; NonSpin HE20=$15.71, HE21=$17.00; ECRS HE20=$10.30. Not offered at HE20-21 (energy discharge priority).

strategy: Stack A — Starting-SOC Overnight AS + Solar Trough Top-Up Charge (HE09-10) + Full Dual-Hour Peak Discharge (HE20-21). Starting SoC 100 MWh enables Non-Spin DA offering HE01-08 (unlike prior day at 0 MWh). Charge to 200 MWh in HE09-10 ($19.21-19.23 DA, solar trough). Hold SoC 200 MWh with Non-Spin (50 MW) + ECRS (30 MW) HE11-18. Shift to Non-Spin + RRS at HE19 ($32.91 energy forfeited — AS wins at this price level). Full 100 MW DA discharge HE20 ($50.98) + HE21 ($53.57) — both higher than yesterday's peak ($52.85). End-of-day SoC: 0 MWh.
spread_convention: spread = DA - RT. Positive = DA expensive = short DA / long RT signal. HE20-22: spread +$12-15 (DA>>RT per market-analyst). DA discharge confirmed by P(DA>RT) 66-67%.

HE01.notes: SoC=100 MWh. Starting SOC enables Non-Spin DA offering from HE01 (key advantage vs prior day 0 MWh start). Non-Spin 50 MW at ~$1/MWh est. DA energy $28.00 (interpolated, overnight shoulder). No energy action — overnight price not cheap enough to charge (HE09 solar trough $19.21 is the target). Wind high (AG2 13,709-14,136 MW overnight).

HE02.notes: SoC=100 MWh. Non-Spin 50 MW continued. Overnight price $26.50 vs solar trough $19.21 — $7.29 premium. No charge.

HE03.notes: SoC=100 MWh. Load minimum HE04 = 46,521 MW (Yes Energy). Non-Spin 50 MW. No charge.

HE04.notes: SoC=100 MWh. System load trough ~46,521 MW. Non-Spin 50 MW. $25.00 estimated — still $5.77 above solar trough. No charge.

HE05.notes: SoC=100 MWh. Morning shoulder building. Non-Spin 50 MW. No charge.

HE06.notes: SoC=100 MWh. HE06=$31.40 (market-analyst production). Morning shoulder before solar onset. Non-Spin 50 MW. $31.40 vs $19.21 = $12.19 premium over solar trough. Confirmed no charge.

HE07.notes: SoC=100 MWh. Solar onset — COPHSL 209 MW at HE07 (market-analyst). Net load still elevated. Non-Spin 50 MW. Price softening ahead of solar ramp. No charge.

HE08.notes: SoC=100 MWh. Solar ramping fast — 13,238 MW by HE09. Price declining toward solar trough. Non-Spin 50 MW (capacity still available — SoC 100 MWh can back Non-Spin). No charge yet — HE09 at $19.21 is the cheapest hour. Hold one more hour.

HE09.notes: PRIMARY CHARGE HOUR 1. $19.21 DA = LOWEST DA PRICE of day (market-analyst HE10 production). Solar 13,238 MW COPHSL and rising fast toward HE13 peak. Net load trough ~29,114-29,331 MW (Yes Energy). SoC 100 -> 185 MWh (100 MW x 0.85 RTE = +85 MWh). DA charge locks in $19.21 regardless of RT congestion. WEST_TO_NORTH LOW-MEDIUM (12-18%) binding risk — DA charge protects against RT discount at GKS. Non-Spin suspended during charge (100 MW fully committed to charging).

HE10.notes: TOP-OFF CHARGE. 18 MW x 0.85 = 15.3 MWh -> SoC 185 -> 200 MWh (cap). $19.23 DA — nearly identical to HE09, second cheapest hour. Net load ~29,114-29,331 MW (trough). P(DA<RT) = 65-66% (market-analyst) — RT likely higher than DA at HE10, making DA charge even more favorable. WEST_TO_NORTH congestion ongoing but DA charge is immune. Charge completes at HE10 end.

HE11.notes: Charge complete. SoC=200 MWh (full). Begin AS offer stack: Non-Spin 50 MW + ECRS 30 MW = 80 MW total AS. Remaining 20 MW buffer. ECRS added post-charge as SoC is confirmed full — ECRS requires discharge capability. DA energy $19.23 far below discharge target ($50+). Net load still near trough. WEST_TO_NORTH congestion possibly ongoing — Non-Spin and ECRS are hub-settled DA products, immune to nodal basis.

HE12.notes: SoC=200 MWh. Solar cresting toward HE13 peak (23,305-24,423 MW). Net load recovering slowly. AS offering: Non-Spin 50 MW + ECRS 30 MW. West congestion easing as wind recovers from HE09 trough.

HE13.notes: SoC=200 MWh. Solar peak HE13 = 23,305 MW (Yes) / 24,423 MW (Enverus). Resource cap-out peaks at 17,979 MW (highest of day). AS offering continues. Price $23.00 well below discharge target. WEST_TO_NORTH easing (wind recovering 6,974 MW at HE14 Enverus).

HE14.notes: SoC=200 MWh. Solar declining from HE13 peak. Net load rising. Wind recovering (6,974 MW Enverus HE14). WEST_TO_NORTH easing. AS offering: Non-Spin 50 MW + ECRS 30 MW. ECRS value ticking up as reserve margin tightens with load ramp.

HE15.notes: SoC=200 MWh. Load building into evening peak. Solar declining. AS value rising with net load ramp. Non-Spin 50 MW + ECRS 30 MW. Cap out 16,726-17,506 MW — reserve margin tightening.

HE16.notes: SoC=200 MWh. Load peak approaching HE17 (70,890 MW). Net load ramp accelerating. AS values stepping up. Non-Spin 50 MW + ECRS 30 MW = 80 MW AS. 20 MW buffer maintained.

HE17.notes: SoC=200 MWh. ERCOT load peak HE17 = 70,890 MW (Yes Energy). Solar 18,940 MW still significant but declining sharply. Net load Enverus 43,110 MW. AS Non-Spin + ECRS at pre-peak elevated value. DA energy $29.50 — not a discharge trigger. Hold SoC.

HE18.notes: SoC=200 MWh. Ramp window entering. Solar collapsing (18,940 MW at HE17 -> ~5,000 MW by HE19). Net load ramp +12,941 MW by HE21 (Enverus). AS Non-Spin + ECRS still offered — energy at $30.00 remains below discharge threshold. HOUSTON_IMPORT MEDIUM (20-28%) starting to build. GKS not directly affected. Final AS offering before switching to RRS at HE19.

HE19.notes: KEY AS HOUR. Energy $32.91 — NOT a discharge trigger (HE20=$50.98 is $18.07 higher; dispatching 100 MW at HE19 vs HE21 costs $2,066). HOLD FULL SOC 200 MWh for HE20-21. Switch AS product: ECRS -> RRS 30 MW (RRS DA $11.28 from market-analyst Smartbidder, confirmed production data). Non-Spin 50 MW at ~$12/MWh est (pre-spike ramp). Total AS = 80 MW. RRS $11.28 > ECRS est for this hour. Yesterday's RRS cleared $510 concentrated HE19-24 — pattern confirmation. P(DA>RT) not specified for HE19 but market-analyst cites HE19-22 as the scarcity window.

HE20.notes: DISCHARGE HOUR 1. 100 MW DA energy at $50.98. SoC 200 -> 100 MWh. P(DA>RT) = 66-67% (market-analyst, Smartbidder composite) — DA expected value higher than RT. Net load ~50,000 MW, GR_WEST declining to ~4,700 MW. Reserve tight (cap out 16,726 MW). All 100 MW committed to DA energy — AS at HE20 (NonSpin $15.71, RRS $11.28, ECRS $10.30) is clearly suboptimal vs $50.98 energy by $35-40/MWh. No AS.

HE21.notes: PRIMARY DISCHARGE HOUR. $53.57 DA = HIGHEST PRICE OF DAY. 100 MW full capacity. SoC 100 -> 0 MWh. Net load peak 53,062 MW (Yes Energy) / 56,051 MW (Enverus). Solar collapsed to ~264 MW. GR_WEST ~3,563 MW. Cap out 16,726-16,835 MW. P(DA>RT) = 66-67%. Non-Spin DA $17.00, ECRS $11.48 at HE21 — irrelevant, energy wins by $36-42/MWh. All 100 MW to DA energy.

HE22.notes: SoC=0 MWh after HE21 full discharge. Cannot discharge. $40.54 DA would be a strong price but no capacity available. Wind building (AG2 11,104-11,426 MW HE22-24). Non-Spin would require SoC — not available. Idle.

HE23.notes: SoC=0. Wind recovering strongly. Price declining from peak. Idle.

HE24.notes: SoC=0. Overnight wind high (GR_WEST 3,563 MW still declining; AG2 system high). Price ~$28.00. Idle. End-of-day SoC = 0 MWh.

summary.strategy_label: Stack A — Overnight AS Hold (HE01-08) + Solar Trough Top-Up (HE09-10) + Mid-Day AS Hold (HE11-19) + Dual Peak Discharge (HE20-21)

da_as_revenue_detail.note: HE01-08 and HE11-18 AS prices estimated (market-analyst confirms off-peak near-zero $1-2/MWh). HE19 RRS $11.28 from Smartbidder (production). HE19 NonSpin $12/MWh estimated (pre-spike ramp).

summary.net_expected_revenue_note: Energy arbitrage $8,188 + AS estimate $2,138. AS HE01-18 component low confidence; HE19 RRS medium confidence.
summary.range_basis: Low: AG2 wind correct (+1,900 MW vs consensus) reduces HE20-21 clearing to $30-40 (energy revenue -$3,000+) plus AS misses. High: RT spike at HE20-21 partially captured if DA clears above forecast, or exceptional ECRS/RRS clearing pre-peak.

congestion_adjustments_applied.HE09_10_charge: DA charge election immunizes against WEST_TO_NORTH RT discount at GKS (P 12-18% binding). No schedule change needed.
congestion_adjustments_applied.HE20_21_discharge: HOUSTON_IMPORT constraint MEDIUM (20-28%) does not affect GKS (West node). No LMP adjustment required. Evening discharge confirmed.
congestion_adjustments_applied.HE01_08_nonspin: Hub-settled DA product — immune to any nodal congestion at GKS.

prior_day_learning_applied.nonspin_dominance: 2026-05-24 NonSpin cleared 980 MW-hr ($1,448). Extended NonSpin offering HE01-08 (enabled by starting SoC=100 MWh vs prior day 0 MWh).
prior_day_learning_applied.rrs_peak_adjacent: 2026-05-24 RRS cleared HE19-24 ($510). RRS 30 MW added at HE19 in place of ECRS.
prior_day_learning_applied.starting_soc_advantage: Starting at 100 MWh (vs 0 MWh on 05-25 schedule) adds ~1,600 in energy revenue (200 MWh discharge at HE20-21 vs 185 MWh) and ~$400 overnight AS.

data_quality_flags: AS_PLAYBOOK_MISSING: recommend_as_position.py output 2026-05-26.json not present. Self-optimization only.
AS_PRICES_HE01_18_ESTIMATED: Off-peak Non-Spin and ECRS prices estimated at $1-5/MWh seasonal proxy. Market-analyst confirms near-zero pattern. Low confidence.
AS_HE19_NONSPIN_ESTIMATED: NonSpin $12/MWh at HE19 estimated. RRS $11.28 is production (Smartbidder).
STARTING_SOC_UNCONFIRMED: 100 MWh assumed per task specification. Operations to verify Tenaska PTP HSL/PMin before 10:00 CT.
HSL_ASSUMED: HSL=100 MW assumed. Operations to confirm from Tenaska PTP dataPoints=[HSL].
AG2_WIND_BULLISH_RISK: AG2 +1,900 MW vs consensus is largest swing factor. Monitor GR_WEST actuals at 05:00 CT.
